systems.name: GrapheneOS
systems.summary: A privacy- and security-hardened Android distribution officially supported on Google Pixel devices. GrapheneOS ships with no Google apps or telemetry by default, offers sandboxed Google Play as an optional compatibility layer, and uniquely lets the owner re-lock the bootloader with their own signing key. Portability is limited by design to the Pixel line.
highlights: Bootloader re-lockable with a custom verified-boot key — owner retains full control after flashing.
No Google apps, services or telemetry by default; connectivity checks point to GrapheneOS-run servers or can be disabled.
Network permission toggle denies any app direct and indirect network access.
Fully open source under a permissive license; all code hosted publicly on GitHub.
Reproducible builds documented against the AOSP build system.
Sandboxed Google Play compatibility layer runs Play Services as an unprivileged, user-installable app.
concerns: Official production builds are limited to a narrow range of Pixel devices.
Root shell is not available by default, by design — may not suit some power-user workflows.
Release binaries are signed with private keys, so bit-for-bit reproducibility requires comparing unsigned outputs.

evidence.note: Bootloader unlock and re-lock with custom key
Open-source project with code on GitHub
Connectivity checks via GrapheneOS servers by default
No Google services by default; sandboxed Play optional
No Google apps or services by default
Officially supports Pixel 6 through Pixel 10 Pro Fold

systems.name: iOS
systems.description: Apple's proprietary mobile OS, shipped exclusively on iPhone hardware and distributed through the App Store.

systems.name: Android (Stock on Pixel)
systems.description: AOSP with Google Mobile Services, as shipped on Google's Pixel hardware.

systems.name: Samsung One UI
systems.description: Samsung's Android skin on Galaxy devices, layering Samsung services on top of the Google stack.

systems.name: HarmonyOS
systems.description: Huawei's mobile operating system, shipped on Huawei and Honor devices with an independent app ecosystem distinct from Google Play and the App Store.

systems.name: LineageOS
systems.description: Community-maintained Android distribution that extends the life of devices beyond their vendor end-of-support.

systems.name: Windows 11
systems.description: Microsoft's flagship desktop OS, integrated with Microsoft accounts, Store and cloud services.

systems.name: macOS Sequoia
systems.description: Apple's desktop OS for Mac hardware, integrated with iCloud and the Mac App Store.

systems.name: ChromeOS
systems.description: Google's cloud-first desktop OS, centered on Chrome, Google services and managed devices.

systems.name: Ubuntu
systems.description: Canonical-backed Linux distribution, among the most widely used desktop Linux platforms, with commercial support and the Snap packaging ecosystem.

systems.name: Fedora Workstation
systems.description: Red Hat-sponsored, community-governed Linux distribution with a leading-edge release cadence.

systems.name: Arch Linux
systems.description: Rolling-release, minimalist Linux distribution configured entirely by the user.

systems.name: Debian Stable
systems.description: Long-running, community-governed Linux distribution known for its conservative release policy and the Debian Free Software Guidelines.
